articles.title: Reliability and clinical utility of paediatric ankle fracture classifications : a systematic review.
articles.authors: Teo LS, Xiao L, Yeo BKK, Rayapati R, Lim AKS, Hoipo Hui J, et al.
articles.study_type: Systematic review of reliability and clinical utility studies
articles.outcomes: Inter- and intraobserver reliability of classification systems; predictive value for premature physeal closure and angular deformity
articles.take_home: The Dias-Tachdjian classification system was most reliable for helping doctors agree on how to classify children's ankle fractures, whilst the Salter-Harris system was most commonly used and best at predicting whether the growth plate would close early. Using both types of classification together may help predict outcomes and plan treatment better.

articles.title: Long-term Outcomes After First Metatarsophalangeal Joint Arthrodesis: With Focus on Degenerative Changes on Adjacent Joints.
articles.authors: Recheis S, Mattausch D, Kuehn N, Kindermann H, Ortmaier R, Bischofreiter M, et al.
articles.population: Patients who underwent first metatarsophalangeal joint arthrodesis for painful end-stage degenerative, inflammatory, post-traumatic conditions or severe deformities
articles.sample_size: 77 patients (45 female, 32 male) followed up at mean 106.9 months
articles.outcomes: Radiographic osteoarthritis progression in adjacent joints (interphalangeal, tarsometatarsal, naviculocuneiform, talonavicular); clinical outcomes measured by AOFAS HMI score, FAOS, FFI; patient satisfaction; revision rates
articles.take_home: First metatarsophalangeal joint fusion provides excellent long-term satisfaction and function, with 91% of patients reporting good or excellent results. Whilst arthritis can develop in nearby joints over time, particularly in the joint just below the fused area, this did not affect how well patients actually did or their satisfaction with the surgery.

articles.title: Multiplanar Stability After Syndesmotic Screw Fixation vs Deltoid Ligament Repair in a Cadaveric Weber B/SER4b Ankle Fracture Model With Fibular Plate Fixation.
articles.authors: Haanaes EKO, Dalen AF, Gregersen MG, Skrede A, Molund M
articles.population: Fresh frozen human cadaveric ankle specimens
articles.outcomes: Talar shift (lateral translation), valgus tilt, and internal and external rotation stability measured under 45-N axial load
articles.take_home: Both syndesmotic screw fixation and deltoid ligament repair restored sideways ankle stability when combined with fibular plate fixation, but deltoid ligament repair alone better restored the ankle's ability to resist tilting and twisting movements.

articles.title: Patient acceptable symptom state for European Foot & Ankle Society Score After Hallux Valgus Surgery.
articles.authors: Lee HJ, Yew KSA, Yeo W, Mohamed Buhary KS, Toh RX, Li Z, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using prospectively collected data
articles.population: Patients who underwent hallux valgus (bunion) surgery
articles.sample_size: 361 patients (324 female, 37 male) followed for 2 years
articles.outcomes: Patient acceptable symptom state (PASS) thresholds for EFAS Score at 6 months (20.5) and 2 years (23.5) postoperatively; AOFAS PASS thresholds at 6 months (84.5) and 2 years (82.5)
articles.take_home: Researchers have identified the scores on the EFAS questionnaire that represent acceptable symptom improvement after bunion surgery, which can help evaluate future treatments. The study found that patients typically needed to reach scores of 20.5 at six months and 23.5 at two years to consider their symptoms acceptable.

articles.title: EFAS Score - validation of the Norwegien version by the Score Committee of the European Foot and Ankle Society (EFAS).
articles.authors: Richter M, Agren PH, Besse JL, Coester M, Kofoed H, Maffulli N, et al.
articles.population: Patients with foot and ankle pathologies undergoing surgery
articles.outcomes: Internal consistency (Cronbach's Alpha 0.86), Standard Error of Measurement (0.31), responsiveness (effect size 1.05), 77% of patients showed improvement in EFAS score
articles.take_home: The Norwegian version of the EFAS Score is a reliable and responsive tool for measuring foot and ankle surgical outcomes. It successfully measured improvement in most patients over 3 to 6 months after surgery.
